name: neural-weights-en-us
model_lineage: v1.5.0-fr-order / step 40000 — the fr.house_number RECOVERY model. Identical to v4.5.0's v1.4.0-charoffset (char-offset corpus v0.5.0, span bridge RETIRED) EXCEPT one added lever: the synth-fr-order shard (#560/#561, weight 3.0), which teaches reversed-order (postcode-first) FR so the model stops grabbing the leading postcode as the house number. RESULT: fr.house_number 54.5 → 87.4 on the diversified golden (#563), +32.9pp — at the published frontier for reordered house numbers (Chinese flexible-order SOTA ~90-91%, Li et al. NAACL 2019). The fr.house_number gate floor was re-baselined 91 → 85 (STATED, operator-approved, research-anchored — docs/articles/evals/2026-06-13-fr-house-number-threshold-research.md + #564): the 91 bar was a canonical-order bar mis-applied to the hard postcode-first slice. Bridge stays retired (po_box 90.3 bridge-OFF). Gate: scripts/eval/gates/v0.5.0-bridge.json; tokenizer 0.6.0-a0.
phase: v1.5 fr-order — recover the bridge-retirement casualty: reversed-order FR shard lifts fr.house_number +32.9pp to the reordered-case frontier

training.corpus_version: 0.5.0 (char-offset span labels #519: from-source base + 7 re-emitted parity overlays) + synth-fr-order shard (#561)
training.recipe: v1.5.0-fr-order: from-scratch 40k on the char-offset corpus + the synth-fr-order shard (weight 3.0, the single variable vs v1.4.0-charoffset). All else held identical (augment_glue_prob 0.25, synth-intersection 1.0, synth-po-box-cedex 1.5, synth-german 6.0, affix relabel, gazetteer anchor + choreography, CE-only, lr=1.5e-4, seed 42). NOTE: weight 6.0 (v1.5.1) was tested and REJECTED — worse (84.7%) + postcode fragmentation (#564); 3.0 is the shipped weight.

requires.$comment: The SHIP-CONFIG this model was TRAINED against (#718). The canonical ProductionScorer (@mailwoman/neural's createScorer) reads this and FAILS CLOSED if a required channel isn't actually fed — closing the #566/#685 trap where a channel-trained model scored channel-OFF silently goes OOD. Read off the gate it shipped against (scripts/eval/gates/v0.5.0-bridge.json: requires_gazetteer_lexicon=true, requires_conventions=auto, requires_bridge=false) plus anchor (the model exports anchor_features). Additive metadata only.

notes: v4.6.0 — recover the v4.5.0 fr.house_number casualty without losing bridge retirement. The synth-fr-order shard (#561, weight 3.0) teaches reversed-order (postcode-first) FR ("47110 Locality, 69 Street"), which the canonical-order BAN training never showed. On the diversified golden (#563, 56 localities, both orders) fr.house_number recovers 54.5 → 87.4 (+32.9pp). It does NOT reach the OLD 91 floor — but that floor was a canonical-order bar mis-applied to the hard reordered slice (research: docs/articles/evals/2026-06-13-fr-house-number-threshold-research.md, #564). Literature puts the reordered-house-number ceiling at ~90-91% (Chinese flexible-order SOTA, Li et al. NAACL 2019) and shows neural parsers COLLAPSE on reorder (deepparse 100→28%, libpostal 0.992→0.781); 87.4% is at the frontier. Floor re-baselined 91 → 85 (STATED, operator-approved). The bridge stays RETIRED (po_box 90.3 bridge-OFF). The NEXT lever to push past 90% is postcode-anchor span protection (reuses the existing anchor signal) — NOT more synth weight: weight 6.0 (v1.5.1) was WORSE (84.7%) and fragmented the postcode (47110 → hn 4 + pc 7110). KNOWN: int8_delta.us.street_prefix flagged 2.2pp (>1.5 cap) — WAIVED: int8 98.0 vs fp32 95.8 is int8 BETTER on a 25-example tag (single-example favorable quant noise, not a regression). Follow-up: corpus-v0.5.1 code-point offset re-align (#558).

format.model: ONNX int8 dynamic (quantized from fp32, max fp32↔int8 delta 0.3pp)

calibration.method: isotonic-regression (PAVA) over per-span softmax confidence; OPT-IN via core/decoder createCalibrator
calibration.note: calibration.json + calibration-per-locale.json carried forward from v4.4.0 (architecture + label space unchanged). Re-fit recommended next train. Apply via @mailwoman/core/decoder's createCalibrator; default parse output is byte-stable when omitted.

gate_2026_06_13_fr_order.promotion_gate: PASS 17/17 quality floors (gates/v0.5.0-bridge.json, bridge-OFF; fr.house_number floor re-baselined 91→85, STATED $comment_change_3). fr.house_number 87.4 ≥ 85. arena.perturb 78 ≥ 71 (gate-integrity fix: the floor was previously un-evaluable, see promotion-gate.sh). bridge-retirement HOLDS: po_box 90.3 bridge-OFF ≥ 89.1. The single non-quality flag — int8_delta.us.street_prefix 2.2 > 1.5 — is WAIVED (int8 98.0 > fp32 95.8 on a 25-example tag = favorable single-example quant noise).

vs_4_5_0_actual.fr_house_number: +32.9 on the diversified golden (54.5 → 87.4) — the recovery; at the reordered-case SOTA frontier (~90-91% ceiling). NOTE: v4.5.0's card listed 89.3 on the EASIER pre-#563 golden; on the same diversified golden v4.5.0 is 54.5%.
vs_4_5_0_actual.note: fr.house_number recovered without disturbing the spine; weight 6.0 (v1.5.1) was rejected (worse + postcode fragmentation, #564). Grade vs the diversified golden #563, not the pre-#563 numbers.

capabilities.$comment: Per-tier (server=anchor+gazetteer; pocket=anchor-only) × address-system × tag capability manifest (#718/#719). maskOffF1 = measured per-tag exact-match F1 with the conventions mask OFF; maskOnF1 = the same with mask ON (recorded only for tags some codex forbiddenTags row suppresses — the loader's delta-gate consults only those). createScorer FAILS CLOSED when a conventions row forbids a tag with maskOffF1 − maskOnF1 > 0.05 (the mask provably destroys a real capability). Generated by scripts/eval/gen-capability-manifest.ts against the v1.5.0 int8.
